feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Analytics Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP Analytics Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Content package: Solution Manager Test Suite Analytics
entry.content:
Dear Colleagues 

I am unsuccessfully trying to leverage the SAP content package created for Solution Manager Test Suite Analytics Solution Manager Test Suite Analytics | SAP Help Portal.

I have imported the content package in my SAC but I cannot connect it to my Solution Manager. I stablished the connection between SAC and BW but data is not refreshed. I do not find anything help in SAP pages and I opened a case to SAP but not getting any guidance.

Have any of you configured this? Any suggestion?

Thanks

Manuel

entry.title: Get the contracts list whose value is greater than 1 million & between 500k to 1 Mil Using Radio But
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

I have a Requirement, Where user wants to get the list of Orders whose value is greater than 1 Million, 500k-1Million, 100K-500K and <100K using a Radio button. Below is the sample table data. I have introduced a Radio button as below.

What I'm not sure is that how could I achieve this using Java Script in SAC.

Regards,

Ram

entry.title: SAC Planning
entry.content:
Planning Data entered at higher node should distribute as per percentage of Actuals.

 

Ex: . When data entered in planning table(for Feb 2025) at  higher node it should split according to the percentage of actual value of (feb 2024).

entry.title: SAP SAC Not Showing Description from OData v4 Metadata
entry.content:
Hi SAP Experts,

I have a custom OData v4 endpoint that includes the sap:label property in its metadata. I am trying to pass descriptions to SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) while creating a model. However, the descriptions are not being displayed in SAC based on my metadata.

I have tried using the following annotations:

  • Org.OData.Core.V1.Description
  • Org.OData.Core.V1.LongDescription
  • sap:label
  • sap:Heading
  • sap.QuickInfo
  • Core.Description

Despite these efforts, SAC does not seem to recognize the description fields. I am testing this in an SAP Analytics Cloud Trial Environment.

Environment:

  • OData Version: 4.0
  • SAC Environment: Trial

Has anyone successfully resolved this issue or encountered a similar challenge? Any guidance or best practices would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: % Calculation in SAC Table
entry.content:
Hi All,

Could someone suggest me on how the below percentage values calculation is achieved? I used %GrandTotal(), but didn't work.

Thanks in advance.

 

   CODE X CODE Y CODE Z 
 Count of Incidents CountRateCountRateCountRate
Code A3 267%133%--
US1 133%----
CANDA2 133%133%  
CHINA        
Code B1     1100%
US        
CANDA        
CHINA      1100%
Code C 1   1100% 
US 1   1100% 
CANDA        
CHINA

entry.title: How to filter a bar graph by dimensions from the dropdown option via scripting?
entry.content:
Hi,

Here is the scenario. I have created a bar graph and user wants to filter this graph by either of the three dimension (Brand, Region, Company Code). I know we can create a standard dimension input control which will serve this requirement but due to some constraint I cannot use the dimension input control. Is there a way, I can create a dropdown and filter the bar graph using the values from this dropdown via scripting. I'm looking at other places also but mostly people are using the dropdown to filter by measures instead of dimension.

Anyone who has faced this similar issue or knows the workaround in this scenario. Their comments would be deeply appreciated.

entry.title: SAC - Export multi-page PDF when using responsive story
entry.content:
Hi all,

I've created a responsive SAC-story with multiple charts and tables. So you have to scroll to see the whole content. Now I want to export it as PDF. 

Unfortunately, only the visible area is exported. I have found various posts that suggest turning on pagination for tables. However, I don't have such option in the responsive story. Is such an export generally not possible in this type of story?

Many thanks
Steffen

entry.title: AUTH - Allow Planners to change dimension attributes only through programmed button in a Story
entry.content:
Hello SAC community,

I have an Authorization topic that I would like to run through you. We have programmed a process in SAC Application Design that allows end users to change dimension attributes via buttons (f.e. CAPEX application where users can create and edit CAPEX projects). This is working fine. But we would like to somehow restrict the users from going directly into the planning model and having the ability to change the dimension attributes right there and save it.

I am not sure if it is even possible. Once the user has the rights to maintain a dimension he can do it right in the model itself. But once we take of these rights, he is not able to create / edit projects in the CAPEX application.

Is there any authorization concept in SAC that you can think of, that could solve this problem? (Allow the user to somehow update the dimension attributes via the application but restrict him from updating the attributes in the planning model itself)

Thank you very much!
Adam

entry.title: Subject Name Identifiier in IAS
entry.content:
Hello all,

I have a question regarding the Subject Name Identifier in IAS.

I’ve been experimenting in my test environment and here's what I did:

  • In Azure, I created a Claim employee_id=user.employee_id.
  • In IAS, I set the Subject Name Identifier to Corporate Identity Provider=employee_id.
  • The user was created in SAC/BTP with the User Name being the employee_id (User Name filed = 131414)

Then I changed the employee_id in Azure, but the user was still able to log in.

Only when I removed the email=user.email claim in Azure (ODIC), the user could no longer log in to BTP.

I thought the SNI was the main factor for identifying the user in the cloud app.

So, for example, if the user with SNI=234324 is sent to BTP, and only the user with SNI (User Name in Cloud App) 1111 exists in BTP, the user should no longer be able to log in – but this is not the case. It seems that, even though the setting in IAS - Subject Name Identifier=employee_id , the user is identified/found by the email address in BTP

Why is that? Or did I misunderstand the purpose of the Subject Name Identifier?

Do I understand it correctly that the Subject Name Identifier is always equal to the User Name in a cloud app (SNI = User Name in SAC)

Thank you very much.

Best Regards

entry.title: Unable to find SubTotal function in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hi All,

Can you please suggest me on how to calculate the below Total_Count value using script. 

Once i get the total_count value, i have to calculate the  % values for each code. Hence i cannot use Show Totals option for that Dimension.

Thanks in advance.

 

 Dimension2Code XCode YCode Z 
 MeasuresCountCountCountTotal_Count
Dimension1Country Dimension    
Code A 21-3
 US1-- 
 CANDA11  
 CHINA    
Code B  437
 US  2 
 CANDA 21 
 CHINA 2  
Code C 1  1
 US    
 CANDA1   
 CHINA

entry.title: Data Analyzer URL Parameter Rows / Columns
entry.content:
After hours of trying I managed to find the right syntax to open a Data Analyzer from Datasphere:

NavigationUtils.openDataAnalyzer("YYY","[XXX][][" + Table_1.getDataSource().getInfo().modelName +"]", [UrlParameter.create("systemType", "DATASPHERE"),UrlParameter.create("autoSubmit", "true")]);

YYY = the name of the connection to the datasphere. It can be found in the SAC menu under connections.

XXX = Datasphere Space Name in which the Analytical Model is located. The name can be found in the Datasphere menu under Spaces.

slightly unrelated:
if one wants to open an existing data analzyer via SAC script:

NavigationUtils.openDataAnalyzer("&/dz/TTT");

TTT = alphanumeric string from the data anlyzer url you want to open.

Now regarding my question. Does anyone know whether there are URL Parameters to tell the openDataAnalyzer which dimensions and measures to put into the rows and columns?

entry.title: Access for users to update allocation rules in SAC
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have a requirement from business for allocation setup in SAP Analytics Cloud. Users want to do the allocation for fixed cost plan data in SAC using the standard allocation process. In the current application it is handled through universal allocations in S/4. The requirement from business side is that users should be able to update the rules (change source, target, driver filters) in the allocation step in data action as per need for each cycle. As it is a technical change in data action configuration, we understand that users will need developer kind of access with edit permission for data action. Also if there are any changes, that needs to be done in development system and transported to production system. There is some risk associated with this from security side. 

There are 2 concerns:

  1. What access will be needed for users to update allocation rules? Is there any other way without giving developer role?
  2. Is it possible to update rules directly in production system?

Could you please guide us on how to proceed with requirement?

entry.title: Why "Just Ask" feature not available for Neo tenant?
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Can someone explain why the "Just Ask" feature is not available for Neo tenants in SAP SAC? 

Does this mean it will be introduced in the future, or is there a specific reason it's not available?

Regards,

Vijai Muniraj

entry.title: SAC - Read description of hierarchy node from variable via script
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I have created a SAC story based on a BW Live connection. A cost center hierarchy node (variable) must be selected in the associated prompt. I would like to output the text/description belonging to the node in a text field using a script. Unfortunately, I can't do that. When I look at the content of the variable in the input prompt, I only get the variable name and value. Is there a way to get the associated description? I tried a lot without success.

Best regards

Steffen

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud Excel Add-in Table Default Number Format
entry.content: When I create a table for one of my data models in excel the default number format is showing four decimals.  I have checked the style in excel and the style for that type of cell says two decimals but whenever I create a table is shows the four decimals.  I know that I can apply that excel style or I can change the table's number format to two decimals but would love if I could save myself the time and have two decimals be the default instead of four.  Every document I have found published by SAP on the topic notes that the default format can be set in the model preferences but it never says where that is.  Can someone point me in the right direction to change that?

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud - Allocation Step - Planning Standard User Maintenance
entry.content:
I noticed a conflict about allocation maintenance in SAP Analytics Cloud. In the user set-up

SAC allows to non-Planning Professional users to create or maintain allocation step.

 

But in the Data Action, SAC allows only Planning Professional users to maintain/Save it.

So, how can a Planning Standard user can maintain an Allocation step if the Data Action can’t be saved from a Planning Standard user?

entry.title: Is there a way to get the values of cells via scripting?
entry.content:
The user would like to be able to show a message on the following conditions (only if value and quantity is not filled):

  • price is 0,
  • price is blank,
  • price is negative or becomes +/-50% more than historical price.

We tried using getSelections() but only dimensions are available.

Is it possible to get measure values via scripting to enable a check as mentioned above?

entry.title: Does SAC have a Rank function?
entry.content:
I did not find the Rank function in the calculation editor when creating a column that simply ranks values in a measure column like below. Please let me know if there is a way to do this in a story in SAC.

Sales             Rank

200                   4

350                   3

500                   2

800                   1

entry.title: SAP HANA Rev 59.16: Driving Innovation with Enhanced Performance, Security, and Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hi SAP HANA Experts, 

I want to create a blog in the portala for powerful in-memory data platform, continues to evolve and play a crucial role in the digital transformation journey for businesses worldwide. The latest release, SPS 05, which reached Revision 59.16 on January 7, 2025, brings a wealth of new features and enhancements aimed at improving performance, security, cloud integration, and user experience. Let’s take a closer look at what’s new in this release and how businesses can benefit from it.

  1. Enhanced Performance and Speed

SAP HANA consistently focuses on improving its core performance, and the Rev 59.16 release continues this trend with significant advancements:

  • Optimized Query Processing: The speed of query execution has been significantly boosted, thanks to improvements in internal memory management. This ensures faster response times, even with complex workloads.
  • Improved Data Tiering: Better data tiering now allows SAP HANA to efficiently manage hot, warm, and cold data. This results in improved system performance, particularly for organizations dealing with massive volumes of data.

These enhancements ensure SAP HANA remains a high-performance solution for real-time analytics, no matter the scale or complexity of the data involved.

  1. Cloud Integration and Hybrid Enhancements

As businesses increasingly embrace hybrid and multi-cloud environments, Rev 59.16 makes it easier for SAP HANA to integrate with various cloud platforms:

  • Cloud-Native Capabilities: The release introduces enhanced cloud-native features, allowing businesses to seamlessly run SAP HANA applications both on-premise and in the cloud, offering greater flexibility.
  • SAP HANA Cloud Integration: Stronger integration tools simplify bridging on-premise SAP HANA with SAP HANA Cloud, creating a smoother hybrid cloud experience and enabling efficient management of data across multiple environments.

These features support a more unified, efficient, and scalable cloud experience for businesses managing their data in a hybrid or multi-cloud infrastructure.

  1. Security Improvements

SAP HANA Rev 59.16 prioritizes enhanced security, addressing the growing concerns around data protection and compliance:

  • Enhanced Data Encryption: Stronger encryption features have been introduced to safeguard sensitive data both in transit and at rest. This ensures that businesses meet stringent security and compliance standards, particularly in industries with high data sensitivity.
  • Stronger Authentication: The system now supports a wider array of authentication protocols, improving access control and security for both users and services interacting with SAP HANA.

These security upgrades ensure that SAP HANA remains a trusted platform for businesses dealing with highly sensitive data and regulatory requirements.

  1. AI and Machine Learning Integration

SAP HANA has also made significant strides in integrating AI and machine learning capabilities, making it easier for businesses to harness the power of advanced analytics:

  • Streamlined AI Integration: The new release enhances integration with AI and machine learning frameworks, enabling businesses to run predictive models directly on their SAP HANA data. This reduces the time between data collection and actionable insights, allowing organizations to make smarter decisions faster.
  • Graph Data Processing: The expansion of graph data processing allows businesses to analyze complex relationships within their data more effectively. This feature is especially valuable in sectors like finance and telecommunications, where understanding intricate data relationships is critical.

These capabilities enable organizations to leverage the full potential of their data and unlock deeper insights using AI and machine learning.

  1. Improved Application Development

For developers working on SAP applications, Rev 59.16 introduces several new tools and features aimed at improving the development experience:

  • New Development Frameworks: The release includes additional development frameworks designed to speed up the application lifecycle, helping developers build and deploy applications more efficiently.

Thanks,

Divya Sahukari

entry.title: Rollingup data for a Dimension in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have a requirement to rollup data in SAP Analytics cloud, based on complaint number, i have to concatenate the code values.

 I couldn't find the way to acheive this using Calculated Dimension, i tried writing the script, but no luck.Could someone please help me on how to acheive this.

 

ComplaintNoCodeProduct name
CN-1A01P1
CN-1E034P1
CN-1F15P1
CN-2A25J1
CN-2G07J1

Output should be like the below.

ComplaintNoCodeProduct name
CN-1A01;E034;F15P1
CN-2A25;G07J1
